page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.large is an x86_64 server instance powered by a 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a total of 2 vCPUs. The system is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU acceleration. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the instance is suited for light, single-threaded workloads. Single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1358.0 and a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1833.19, both falling into the average category. However, multi-core and memory performance are limited. The Geekbench multi-core score is 1584.0, and the stress-ng multi-core score is 1918.58, both rated as poor. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write speeds measured at 14.29 GB/sec and 12.87 GB/sec respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a low-cost entry point for basic computing tasks. Because it lacks local NVMe storage and has a low memory capacity, it is not designed for resource-intensive applications. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent, albeit modest, processing power without the performance fluctuations associated with shared-core burstable instances. However, the 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and poor database and Redis performance present clear bottlenecks for high-throughput applications.

The ecs.u1-c1m2.large is recommended for lightweight utility workloads, simple web serving, single-threaded scripting tasks, and basic development or testing environments. It can also support small-scale text processing or minor background jobs that do not require high memory density or rapid multi-core execution.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.large is an entry-level x86_64 server instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor and 4.0 GB of RAM. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1358.0, but poor multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. Due to these resource constraints, the instance is highly cost-efficient for lightweight, non-intensive workloads. It is recommended for basic web serving, single-threaded background tasks, lightweight utility scripts, and development or testing environments that do not demand high memory or parallel processing power.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.large is a budget-friendly x86_64 instance with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, ideal for lightweight utility and single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly, dedicated dual-core Intel Xeon instance designed for lightweight utility workloads, basic web serving, and development environments.

4.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
